articles.title: Disney Reveals AI Plans for Theme Park Personalization and Labor Forecasting
articles.summary: Disney's new CEO Josh D'Amaro outlined plans to use AI to simplify trip planning, personalize guest experiences, and implement precision labor demand forecasting across its theme parks. CFO Hugh Johnston noted the initiative could improve guest experience, employee satisfaction, and cost management simultaneously. FEC operators should take note as these AI-driven strategies for staffing and personalization are increasingly accessible at all venue scales.

articles.title: Raw Thrills Launches Stranger Things Arcade Redemption Game
articles.summary: Raw Thrills has globally launched Stranger Things Arcade, a four-player family-friendly redemption game set across iconic locations from the hit Netflix series. The game taps into one of the most-watched streaming franchises to drive arcade floor engagement. FEC operators looking for a high-profile IP-driven redemption draw should take a close look at this new title.

articles.title: WhiteWater Reports 60+ Project Openings Worldwide in Record Year
articles.summary: WhiteWater, the world's largest water attraction designer and manufacturer, is highlighting breakthrough projects across its brands including Water Parks, FlowRider, Endless Surf, and Vantage as part of a banner year exceeding 60 openings globally. The breadth of projects signals robust capital investment in water-based entertainment worldwide. Operators planning waterpark expansions or upgrades can look to these projects as benchmarks for innovation.

articles.title: Foundations Entertainment University Schedules Tennessee Event for FEC Operators
articles.summary: Foundations Entertainment University, a leading education and training resource for the FEC industry, has announced an upcoming event in Tennessee. These gatherings offer operators practical training on running and growing coin-op amusement and FEC businesses. Attendance can be especially valuable for operators seeking peer networking and business-building strategies.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Announced for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center is coming to Mt. Juliet, Tennessee, adding another venue to the growing FEC landscape in the Nashville metro area. The announcement reflects continued consumer demand and investor confidence in community-based entertainment. FEC operators and developers monitoring market expansion opportunities should watch this market closely.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Named One of USA Today's Most Trusted Brands by Parents
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has earned a spot on USA Today's Most Trusted Brands by Parents list, reinforcing its brand positioning in the family entertainment space. This recognition underscores the importance of brand trust and safety perception in attracting family audiences. Independent FEC operators can use this as a benchmark for how brand reputation influences consumer decisions.

articles.title: Florida Settles Lawsuit Following 6-Year-Old's Death in Go-Kart Crash
articles.summary: Florida has reached a settlement with the family of a 6-year-old child killed in a go-kart crash at an amusement facility. The case highlights the critical importance of safety protocols, age and height restrictions, and equipment maintenance at go-kart attractions. FEC operators running go-kart venues should review their liability coverage and safety procedures in light of this settlement.

articles.title: Amusement Center to Pay $300K Settlement Following Investigation into Girl's Death
articles.summary: An amusement center has agreed to pay a $300,000 settlement following an investigation into the death of a young girl at its facility. The case is a sobering reminder of the legal and financial exposure FEC operators face when safety incidents occur. Operators should ensure robust incident response plans, thorough staff training, and adequate liability insurance are in place.

articles.title: Ellis & Associates Launches AI-Supported Drowning Detection eLearning Course
articles.summary: Ellis & Associates has released a new AI-supported online training course focused on scanning and drowning detection for aquatic safety professionals. The course uses real-time video footage to help lifeguards better recognize distress behaviors in swimmers. FEC operators with waterpark or pool attractions should consider integrating this training to strengthen safety programs.

articles.title: P2:3 Consulting Partners with Aqua Tile to Improve Waterpark Safety and Surfaces
articles.summary: P2:3 Consulting has announced a partnership with Aqua Tile to bring safer, more comfortable, and easier-to-maintain flooring solutions to aquatic facilities and waterparks. The collaboration targets common operator challenges including aging infrastructure, wet-surface safety, and long-term maintenance costs. Waterpark and aquatic FEC operators should evaluate these surfacing solutions as part of renovation or expansion planning.

articles.title: Semantic's LOOP Platform Offers Multi-Brand CMS for Attraction Operators
articles.summary: Semantic has launched LOOP, a single CMS platform that allows attraction operators to manage multiple brands or locations within one digital system, reducing fragmentation and operational complexity. The platform handles bookings, security, and performance centrally while allowing flexible visual customization per brand or location. Multi-location FEC operators could significantly reduce digital overhead by adopting a similar unified content management approach.

articles.title: Kidtopia Indoor Playground with Obstacle Courses and Climbing Coming to Dublin
articles.summary: Kidtopia, a new indoor playground featuring obstacle courses and indoor climbing, is set to open in Dublin, adding to the growing market of active-play FEC concepts. The venue targets families seeking structured physical activity in a supervised indoor environment. This opening reflects the sustained demand for experiential, active-play entertainment centers in suburban markets.

articles.title: Black Bunny FEC Combines Gaming, Adventure, and Family Entertainment in Dubai
articles.summary: A new venue called Black Bunny has opened in Dubai, blending gaming, adventure activities, and family entertainment into a single destination. The Dubai market continues to attract diverse FEC concepts as part of the region's broader push to develop leisure and tourism infrastructure. Global FEC developers can look to Dubai as a testing ground for innovative mixed-use entertainment formats.

articles.title: New FEC 'Gameland' Brings VR, Sim Racing, and Retro Gaming to Cookstown
articles.summary: A new family entertainment venue called Gameland has opened in Cookstown, offering a mix of virtual reality, sim racing, and retro gaming experiences. The concept illustrates how smaller markets can support diverse, technology-driven FEC offerings that blend nostalgia with modern immersive entertainment. Operators considering VR or sim racing additions can look to Gameland's mixed-format approach as a model.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Coral Springs Location Shuts Down
articles.summary: The Monster Mini Golf franchise location in Coral Springs, Florida has permanently closed this week, highlighting continued attrition in the indoor mini golf segment. Franchise closures can reflect a range of factors including lease pressures, post-pandemic foot traffic shifts, and increased local competition. FEC operators in the mini golf space should use closures like this as a prompt to evaluate their own financial health and market positioning.

articles.title: Former Queensbury Amusement Center Eyed for Distillery Conversion
articles.summary: A former amusement center in Queensbury is being considered for conversion into a distillery under newly submitted plans. The shift illustrates how entertainment real estate can be repurposed when FEC concepts fail or leases end. Operators and landlords in the industry should be aware of the growing trend of adaptive reuse of entertainment venue spaces.

articles.title: 23 Jump Street Trampoline and Adventure Venue Opens at Rivergate in Cape Town
articles.summary: 23 Jump Street has opened a new high-energy family entertainment venue at Rivergate in Cape Town, South Africa, bringing trampoline parks and adventure activities to the region. The opening reflects continued international growth of the active-play FEC format beyond traditional Western markets. Trampoline park operators can monitor international openings for programming and design inspiration.

articles.title: Rage Room Concept Set to Open in Kitimat, Canada
articles.summary: A new rage room attraction is set to open in Kitimat, British Columbia, offering guests the experience of smashing objects in a controlled, supervised environment. Rage rooms represent a growing niche within the experiential entertainment category that can complement traditional FEC offerings. Operators looking to diversify their attraction mix with low-capex, high-novelty experiences may find this concept worth exploring.

articles.title: Claw Arcade Facility Heads to City Planning Panel for Approval
articles.summary: A dedicated claw arcade facility is going before a city planning panel, signaling the formalization of a growing entertainment retail concept built around prize claw machines. The claw machine segment has seen a resurgence driven by social media and younger demographics seeking affordable entertainment. FEC operators may want to evaluate dedicated claw or prize redemption zones as a low-overhead attraction add-on.

articles.title: India Emerging as a Major Growth Market for Attractions and FECs
articles.summary: India's rapidly growing middle class, estimated at 500 million and expanding by 310 million over the next decade, is fueling surging demand for quality leisure and entertainment experiences. With 66% of the population under 35, young Indian consumers are increasingly willing to spend on entertainment, creating significant opportunities for FEC developers and operators. Industry experts from FORREC note the market is evolving at multiple scales, from small urban centers to large destination resorts.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia Leads Middle East Tourism Boom, Signaling FEC Opportunity
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia's travel and tourism GDP rose 7.4% to $178 billion, representing 46% of the entire Middle East tourism economy and growing at nearly twice the global average rate. Strong domestic and international visitor spending is creating fertile ground for new entertainment destinations across the kingdom. FEC developers and attraction suppliers should view the Saudi market as a high-priority growth opportunity over the next several years.

articles.title: Amoeba Bengaluru Rebrands as Next-Gen Sports Bar with Entertainment Focus
articles.summary: Amoeba in Bengaluru, India has relaunched as a next-generation sports bar concept, integrating gaming and entertainment into a bar and dining experience. The rebrand reflects the growing trend of blending F&B with interactive entertainment to drive longer dwell times and broader demographics. FEC operators considering hybrid bar-entertainment formats can look to this model as India's urban entertainment scene matures.

articles.title: Bókun Launches Launchpad Competition for Experience Operators in Europe
articles.summary: Bókun, a Tripadvisor-owned booking and channel management platform, is offering European experience operators the chance to win a $10,000 cash prize and a package of distribution, marketing, and technology support through its new Launchpad Competition. The prize includes commission-free distribution on Viator and GetYourGuide for a year along with conference access and professional video production. FEC and attractions operators in Europe with innovative concepts should consider applying before the deadline.

articles.title: Story Land Theme Park Unveils New Attractions and Shows for 2026 Season
articles.summary: Story Land in New Hampshire is launching an all-new lineup for 2026 including new live shows, reimagined experiences, and returning fan-favorite attractions. The park's seasonal refresh strategy demonstrates how consistent annual updates can drive repeat visitation from families. FEC operators can draw lessons from Story Land's approach to layering new programming onto existing infrastructure.

articles.title: Six Flags Announces Executive Leadership Transitions
articles.summary: Six Flags has announced leadership changes at the executive level, a development worth monitoring given the company's scale and influence across the regional amusement park and FEC-adjacent market. Leadership transitions at major operators can signal shifts in strategic priorities including capital investment, technology adoption, and guest experience direction. FEC operators and suppliers who work with or compete near Six Flags properties should stay informed as the new leadership takes shape.

articles.title: Disneyland Moves Toward a Cashless Guest Experience
articles.summary: Disneyland is preparing for a cashless future, joining a broader industry trend toward digital-only payment systems at major attraction venues. The shift reduces cash handling costs and speeds up transactions but also raises questions about accessibility for guests without bank accounts or credit cards. FEC operators considering or already implementing cashless systems can look to Disney's approach as the industry standard evolves.

articles.title: Disney Reaffirms Abu Dhabi Theme Park Plans Despite Regional Uncertainty
articles.summary: The Walt Disney Company confirmed its Abu Dhabi theme park project remains on track despite geopolitical uncertainty in the region, framing it as a long-term capital-light investment using an established local operator model. Disney's experience segment posted strong quarterly results, reinforcing investor confidence in theme park expansion. For FEC suppliers and developers eyeing the Middle East, Disney's commitment signals continued high-level investment in the region's entertainment infrastructure.

articles.title: Lumsden Design Firm Wins King's Award for Enterprise in Retail and F&B for Attractions
articles.summary: Lumsden, a UK-based design firm specializing in retail and food-and-beverage spaces for visitor attractions, has received a King's Award for Enterprise for International Trade, recognizing its global portfolio with clients including Netflix and Warner Bros. The firm's work demonstrates how high-quality retail and F&B design can serve as a core revenue driver within attraction venues. FEC operators planning new builds or renovations should consider the commercial design of retail and dining spaces as a strategic priority.

articles.title: Zamperla Delivers First Family Launch Coaster to Parc Spirou with Naruto IP Theme
articles.summary: Zamperla has supplied its first Family Launch Coaster, themed around the popular Naruto manga IP, to Parc Spirou in France as the anchor of a new 15,000-square-meter themed zone. The integrated ride package includes a Nebulaz model and delivers over 1,000 guests per hour across the zone. FEC and park operators considering new ride investments can look to Zamperla's IP-driven, high-throughput coaster solutions as a compelling attraction anchor strategy.
